✓ trailer-width.test.ts is archive-proof: fixture-rooted reads, vendored bytes diff-identical, assertions unmodified, pre-fix lines preserved as red fixture: grep -cF '.planning' → 0; diff -q clean for results.json, all 8 cols-*.txt, and 43-MEASUREMENT.md vs live originals; diff touches only the path constants (ROOT/PHASE/MEAS/RESULTS/DOC), assertion bodies untouched; 7/7 tests pass; the exact removed lines are preserved verbatim in tests/fixtures/hygiene/live-phases-snippet.txt
✓ D-03 standing red-capable guard in npm test: both historical instances flagged in-suite from provenance-pinned fixtures, live tree clean, allowed reads allowed, allowlist self-verifies: tests/hygiene/live-fixture-guard.test.ts runs in the default vitest glob; R1 and R2 red-capability tests assert scanSource flags the vendored snippets; 'live tree is clean' and 'allowlist entries are live' both pass; all 4 guard tests green

✓ Allowlist honest: config.test.ts excused with reason, staleness asserted (excused pattern must remain), live tree scan clean: ALLOWLIST entry for tests/config/config.test.ts carries a reason; 'allowlist entries are live' test asserts the file exists and still contains both process.cwd() and .tickmarkr, so a stale entry reddens; 'live tree is clean' passes

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- stripLineComment truncates at the first '//' even inside string literals, so a line like join(base, "x//y/.planning/phases/z") or a URL-bearing line would false-negative R1 — acceptable heuristic ceiling, but worth noting
- R2 is file-level co-occurrence of process.cwd() + .tickmarkr, so an unrelated legitimate use of both strings in one file will red the guard and force an allowlist entry (fail-closed, so acceptable)
- vendored-bytes ≡ live-originals equality was verified only at vendoring time per the SUMMARY, not enforced by a standing test — fixtures and .planning copies can silently diverge before v1.12 archives the phase dir (which is the intended endpoint, so low risk)

data.details:
✓ Incident transcript vendored with provenance; two HYG-08 tests RED on unfixed baseline.ts; three pins GREEN on HEAD: Fixture tests/fixtures/baseline-incident/fresh-output.txt vendored; test file cites run-20260711-154920 provenance. Independently verified: restored a92f60f's baseline.ts and ran tests/gates/baseline.test.ts — exactly 2 failed (headline assertion `expected false to be true`, meta.failingTests `undefined`) | 13 passed, so the fail-closed pin, no-headline fallback pin, and fingerprint-unchanged pin are GREEN against unfixed code. On HEAD all 15 pass (full suite 847 passed).
✓ Failing details headline runner's own FAIL line(s) + summary from fresh output, fingerprint diff demoted to labeled secondary section, meta.failingTests carried, byte-identical degradation with no FAIL anchor, verdict flow untouched: headlineDetails() harvests FAIL_ANCHOR_RE/SUMMARY_FAIL_RE lines from `raw` (fresh, cwd-stripped, ANSI-stripped, digits un-normalized), demotes fingerprints under 'new failure fingerprints vs baseline (secondary):', sets meta.failingTests to the FAIL-anchor lines, and returns today's exact `new failures vs baseline:` text with no meta when no anchors match — each behavior pinned by a passing test. Only the fresh.length truthy arm's result construction changed; verdict booleans unchanged.
✓ Headline names `config > MODEL-10` and excludes `# done, # failed`/`fail-closed` noise; proven RED by vendored incident-shape transcript: Test asserts headline contains the full FAIL line naming config > MODEL-10 and `Tests  1 failed | 815 passed (816)` (digits un-normalized), and does NOT contain 'done, 0 failed' or 'fail-closed'; fixture includes the tickmarkr CLI noise and fail-closed test-name lines. Verified RED against unfixed HEAD (assertion failed exactly as claimed), GREEN on HEAD.
✓ Verdict logic byte-untouched: fingerprint(), PASS_LINE_RE, ANSI_RE, renormalize, exit-0 fast pass, forgiven branch, fail-closed branch identical, pinned incl. fail-closed details string and FAIL-line fingerprinting: Diff to src/gates/baseline.ts adds only two regexes, headlineDetails(), and an extract-variable `raw` whose value is byte-identical to the prior fingerprint() argument; fingerprint/PASS_LINE_RE/ANSI_RE/renormalize/fast-pass/forgiven/fail-closed branches and the 'failing closed' details string are unmodified. Pins verify: fail-closed still fails with /failing closed/ and no meta; fingerprint(fixture) still contains the normalized FAIL line (no regex narrowing).
✓ Graceful degradation: no-FAIL-anchor output (tsc-style, crash-before-reporter) yields details byte-identical to today's `new failures vs baseline:` text: headlineDetails returns `new failures vs baseline:\n${fresh.join("\n")}` — the exact prior template — when no anchor lines match; pinned by the tsc-shaped (`error TS2554`) test asserting startsWith('new failures vs baseline:'), no 'failing tests:' prefix, and meta undefined.
✓ GateResult.meta carries failingTests: string[] machine-readably; details remains the only field daemon.ts:445 / consult.ts:32 render; no downstream changes: meta.failingTests holds the raw FAIL-anchor lines (pinned by toEqual([INCIDENT_FAIL_LINE])), following the meta.reviewer precedent. Diff touches only baseline.ts, the test file, the fixture, and a SUMMARY doc — zero daemon.ts/consult.ts changes, and tsc confirms the existing meta shape accepts it.
✓ npm test zero-token, tsc --noEmit exit 0, coverage floors hold, runtime deps exactly picomatch/yaml/zod: Verified directly: npm test → 847 passed | 1 skipped (fake-adapter only, no tokens); npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit → exit 0; npm run test:coverage → exit 0 with all per-glob thresholds satisfied (gates well above 80/70 floors); package.json dependencies are exactly picomatch, yaml, zod.

data.summary: Closed HYG-07: servable-filtered channels are named truths in plan/doctor with reason-carrying pin failures, and stale doctor.json (>24h mtime TTL) now prints its age +refresh command in plan — routing decision byte-unchanged, all 9 coverage floors green, zero new deps.

data.details:
reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Non-blocking: headlineDetails harvests ALL FAIL-anchor lines from fresh raw output, including pre-existing (forgiven-at-baseline) FAIL lines — if a baseline-known failing test coexists with a NEW non-FAIL failure (e.g. a tsc error), the headline names the stale forgiven test while the actual new failure sits only in the demoted secondary section. The plan explicitly accepts this trade (fingerprint-collision risk of set-difference), so it is per-spec, but worth a follow-up note.
- Non-blocking: tests interpolate INCIDENT_FIXTURE (an import.meta.dirname-derived absolute path) unquoted into run.sh (`cat ${INCIDENT_FIXTURE}`) — breaks if the repo checkout path ever contains spaces; quoting it in the heredoc would be free.
- Observation only: SUMMARY_FAIL_RE also matches vitest's ' Test Files  1 failed (1)' line, so headlines can carry two summary lines; harmless and arguably more informative.
exit 0
exit 0

✓ Every servable-dropped channel is a named truth in plan and doctor, unauthed installed adapters named in plan, pin to filtered channel fails with reason, routing decision byte-unchanged: servableExclusions()/servabilityLine() added to registry.ts as pure additions (diff shows discoverChannels body untouched); both plan.ts and doctor.ts print the line when non-empty; plan prints 'installed but unauthed: …'; servableReason() appends the unservable reason to both RoutingError messages and route lints; a parity test asserts servableExclusions names exactly what discoverChannels dropped, and a fixture test asserts the returned channel list is unchanged.
✓ Stale doctor.json cannot masquerade as current truth: over-TTL prints age + refresh command, fresh prints nothing, zero added latency, on-disk schema untouched: plan keeps the `readDoctor ?? probeAll` preference (cached path only gains a statSync); doctorAgeMs() uses file mtime — no new field written anywhere; staleness line fires only when cached && age > 24h; tests cover stale (backdated 26h via utimesSync), fresh (no line), and absent-doctor.json (probeAll fallback, no line).
✓ HYG-07(a) servable exclusions printed in exclusionLine() voice in BOTH plan and doctor, e.g. 'servability: 1 channel(s) unservable — pi:… (not in pi's served model list)'; RED on HEAD: servabilityLine() produces exactly this format; plan.ts and doctor.ts both emit it when non-empty; new tests in plan.test.ts and doctor.test.ts assert the line, the channel key, and the reason text; SUMMARY documents grep-verified 0 occurrences of these strings on HEAD, and the assertions reference code that doesn't exist on HEAD (import-failure RED).
✓ Unauthed half: plan names installed-but-unauthed adapters excluded from routing: plan.ts adds `installed but unauthed: <ids> — channels excluded from routing` computed from health[a.id].installed && !authed; test 'installed-but-unauthed adapters are named in plan' asserts it with a grok fixture.
✓ Pin honesty: unroutable line for a pin targeting a servable-filtered channel names the servability reason, not just 'not available': servableReason() matches the channel key inside the RoutingError message and appends '— <key> is unservable (not in <adapter>'s served model list)' to both the plan line and the lint; the same suffix is applied to route lints for the routingHints.pin degradation path; both cases have dedicated tests asserting 'unservable' appears.
✓ HYG-07(b): plan prefers cache, hardcoded 24h TTL with ponytail: comment naming promote-to-config path, mtime-based zero-schema-change signal, fresh prints nothing: DOCTOR_STALE_MS = 24h with the ponytail: comment naming the promote-to-config path; doctorAgeMs uses statSync mtime and returns null when absent; message text matches the required format; the `if (cached)` guard keeps the probeAll fallback silent; fresh-cache test asserts no line.
✓ Routing DECISION unchanged everywhere: discoverChannels identical, route()/nextChannel() untouched, daemon.ts untouched with the deliberate non-touch noted in SUMMARY: registry.ts diff contains only import additions and three new exported functions — no lines removed or changed inside discoverChannels; router.ts and daemon.ts do not appear in the diff; SUMMARY has a dedicated 'Deliberate non-touch: daemon.ts:57' section explaining the decision; the parity + fixture tests pin the returned channel list.
✓ npm test zero-token, tsc --noEmit exit 0, coverage floors hold (adapters and cli both touched), zero new dependencies: New tests use only FakeAdapter/stubs and tmpdir fixtures (zero-token); SUMMARY reports 858 passed / tsc exit 0 / coverage adapters 96.78/84.92 and cli 100/100 above floors; the diff adds tests for every new branch (attribution, unauthed, both pin paths, stale/fresh/absent staleness, compat no-servable-field); staleness uses node:fs statSync — no dependency changes in the diff.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- minor: servableReason matches via msg.includes(key) — a key that is a string prefix of another channel key (e.g. pi:o3 vs pi:o3-mini) could mis-attribute the reason on the wrong lint line; cosmetic only, routing unaffected
- minor: a channel that is both servable-filtered and allow/deny-excluded can appear in both the exclusionLine and servabilityLine attributions; harmless double-naming

all 7 changed files in scope

✓ Default config: judge/review/consult panes close when output read, merged worker pane closes on done exactly once, forever = keep-everything override, failed attempts keep context, nothing closes a slot it didn't create: daemon.ts adds keepForever/keepLlm (false under default 'run', so llm.ts/consult.ts existing `if (!keep) close(slot)` primitives fire when the result is read), a done-path close that splices the slot out of keptSlots (sweep cannot double-close; indexOf guard covers 'attempt' where the slot was already closed), forever short-circuits both paths, only the successful attempt's slot handle is closed (prior attempts stay in keptSlots for the sweep), and every close targets the created slot handle — each behavior is pinned by a corresponding stub-driver test.
✓ Probe rig cannot leak: scratch --no-focus workspace closed in a finally on every path including errors (unit-proven with stubbed herdrSh), payload no longer trips loop detectors, refusals are honest unmeasurable cells, out-of-repo doctrine handed to operator: scripts/probe-rig.mjs exports withScratchWorkspace (workspace create --no-focus, close in finally, fail-loud if create fails); tests/scripts/probe-rig.test.ts proves close-on-throw, close-on-success, no-close-on-create-failure, sync-body handling, and parseWorkspaceId fail-closed with a recording stub and zero tokens; payload is unique numbered-word padding; LOOP_REFUSAL_RE records { refused: true } and the loop continues; SUMMARY carries the explicit '## Operator follow-up: doctrine outside this repo' section naming the ~/.claude/skills/tickmarkr-auto/SKILL.md edits.
✓ EPHEMERAL PANES SELF-CLEAN: judge/review and consult panes close when read, tested with explicit llm: pane + keepPanes: run fixture, ordered stub driver shows close before merge notification / before next attempt dispatch, RED on unfixed HEAD, forever remains the debug override: Two new tests set 'visibility:\n  llm: pane\n  keepPanes: run' explicitly and use an orderedDriver op stream: judge close index < merging-notify index, and consult close index < attempt-3 worker slot index. On unfixed HEAD keepLlm tracked keepOpen (true under 'run'), so those closes only occurred in the run-end sweep after all downstream ops — the ordering assertions fail RED, as documented with exact failing assertions in the SUMMARY. The forever test pins zero closes.
✓ DONE MEANS GONE: merged task's worker slot closed on the task-done path, stub log shows exactly ONE close (sweep skips removed slots), RED on HEAD, forever pinned, failed/parked panes keep today's behavior: The T1→T2 dependency test asserts T1's worker close precedes T2's slot creation (RED on HEAD where the worker persisted to sweep) and filters the op stream to exactly one close for that slot name (splice from keptSlots prevents the sweep double-close). The forever test pins zero closes; the failed-attempts test proves a0 is closed only later by the sweep, after a1's done-close.
✓ CLOSE ONLY WHAT YOU OWN: every new close targets the closer's own slot handle, never a scan or tab label; with two concurrent tasks A's done-close never closes B's slot (stub-asserted): The daemon code closes only the local `slot` variable via keptSlots.indexOf(slot); the concurrent T1/T2 test (concurrency 2, T2 delayed) asserts the first worker close is T1's own slot name and that each task's slot closes exactly once — a scan would double-close T2 and fail.
✓ PROBE RIG NEVER LEAKS: rig in a dedicated unfocused scratch workspace closed in a finally on every die()/error path (die was process.exit past teardown), lifecycle extracted zero-token-testable, belt-and-braces per-tab close on happy path: measure-trailer-width.mjs wraps the whole rig in withScratchWorkspace; in-rig die() now throws (routing through the finally) with a top-level catch that prints and exits 1 after teardown; pre-rig guards use refuse() before anything is created; tabs target the scratch workspace id, never HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID; the happy-path `tab close` per tab is retained per the A2 caveat; the extracted lifecycle is stub-tested with zero tokens.
✓ OBS-07 folded in: varied numbered-word padding instead of repeated-pattern summary; vendor loop-refusal recorded as unmeasurable cell (refused: true), never retried: LONG_SUMMARY becomes 40 unique numbered words (word-001…word-040); LOOP_REFUSAL_RE matches known refusal signatures, refused is written into the results cell, and the loop simply continues to the next pane with no retry path.
✓ DOCTRINE STAYS DOCTRINE: SUMMARY carries an '## Operator follow-up: doctrine outside this repo' section naming the ~/.claude/skills/tickmarkr-auto/SKILL.md edits; worker does not edit ~/.claude: 44-04-SUMMARY.md contains the exact section with the two doctrine rules (daemons detached, nothing splits the ORCHESTRATOR tab, citing OBS-02), explicitly hands the SKILL.md edit to the operator, and the diff touches nothing outside the repo.
✓ npm test zero-token, tsc --noEmit exit 0, coverage floors hold, zero new dependencies, .tickmarkr/ state and journal formats untouched: All new tests use the fake adapter, SubprocessDriver wrappers, or pure stubbed herdrSh (no tokens, no herdr binary); the diff adds no dependencies and touches no schema/journal/state code (only a config-template comment, daemon keep-values, and scripts/tests); SUMMARY records 857 passed, tsc exit 0, and all 9 coverage floors held, consistent with the diff (src/run touched with 6 new tests, src/drivers untouched).

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Minor: the done-path `await driver.close(slot)` in daemon.ts runs after a successful merge but before `return` — if close() throws (flaky herdr), the dispatch-loop catch would mark an already-merged task as failed; consider try/catching the hygiene close so pane cleanup can never fail a green task.
- Minor: on a loop-refusal the code records `refused` from LOOP_REFUSAL_RE but does not force `parseOk: false` as the SUMMARY claims — in practice a refusal won't emit a valid trailer so parseOk is false anyway, but the invariant is implicit rather than enforced.
- Minor: the 'close only what you own' test depends on `sleep 0.4` timing under concurrency 2 — a heavily loaded CI box could reorder completions and flake; the assertions on exact per-slot close counts still hold, only the firstWorkerClose identity check is timing-sensitive.
- Noted, acceptable: `herdr workspace close` semantics remain assumption A2 (unverified), mitigated by keeping per-tab close as the happy-path cleaner — matches the acceptance criteria's belt-and-braces requirement.
